propositions: The rebar-debug skill's Phase 2 writes code (RED test and fix) but never instructs the operator to isolate that work in a separate worktree.
Reproduction, experiments, and fixes can land in the primary checkout on a stale branch, violating contributor rules.
A setup directive should be added at the very start of skill execution to switch to a fresh worktree based on origin/main.
The instruction must name the concrete mechanism using git worktree add against origin/main.
The change is docs/skill content only and must not alter the two-phase debugging protocol semantics.
